scenario_results.reason: The pack advances a continued-stay case only when behavioral-health-specific eligibility, level-of-care, active-treatment, and reviewer-action artifacts are present.
Missing eligibility and level-of-care evidence are converted into a blocked exception path with minimum-prerequisite requests.
Completion is refused when no controlling reviewer action, notice package, or final authorization state exists.
Behavioral utilization terminology is normalized to named artifacts before any lifecycle progression occurs.
Crisis- or withdrawal-risk facts trigger escalation to licensed or crisis ownership rather than routine utilization handling.
Requests to issue final medical-necessity denials are rejected as outside the lane's administrative-utilization authority.
Conflicting EHR and payer portal status data block the case and route it for reconciliation instead of guessed closure.
Part 2-protected SUD records are not disclosed without an authorized pathway, and the case is escalated for privacy review.
Deficient adverse benefit notices are returned for rework rather than treated as complete utilization outcomes.
Public-versus-tenant criteria conflicts are surfaced as low-confidence escalation with assumptions recorded.
The pack refuses generic prior-auth handling that strips out behavioral-health-specific level-of-care and continuity context.
Step-down or discharge work remains open until continuity-of-care evidence is captured or explicitly handed off.

notes: All twelve scenarios pass the validated threshold with full required ability coverage.
The pack stays behaviorally specific through level-of-care, active-treatment, privacy, notice, and continuity constraints while remaining explicit that runtime is still `specification-based`.
